mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls signal expanding labor income and consumption, which lifts aggregate corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of observations when PAYEMS increases month-over-month, or the rank correlation between PAYEMS change and forward returns is negative over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 1.5%, the real discount rate on future corporate cash flows drops sufficiently to re-rate equity valuations upward, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of instances when FEDFUNDS is below 1.5%, or the mean return in those instances does not exceed the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month rise in the unemployment rate while the federal funds rate exceeds 2.5% indicates restrictive monetary policy hitting a weakening labor market, which depresses aggregate demand expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction term between UNRATE change and FEDFUNDS level lacks statistical significance (p > 0.05) in predicting returns.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP during the early expansion phase signals accelerating aggregate demand that outpaces prior trend, leading to positive earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns following positive GDPC1 growth are not statistically different from zero (t-test p > 0.05) during periods classified as early expansion.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production signals contracting manufacturing activity and inventory drawdowns that compress corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when INDPRO declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation accelerates month-over-month while the federal funds rate remains below 2.0%, negative real interest rates stimulate credit expansion and asset reflation, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL change and FEDFUNDS level does not significantly predict 20-day returns (p > 0.05), or positive returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts below 1.2 million units signals deteriorating residential investment and downstream demand for construction materials and durable goods, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of observations when HOUST is below 1200.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales signals strengthening consumer expenditure that directly lifts top-line revenues for consumer-facing corporations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in RSAFS coincide with negative S&P 500 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of observations.
